name: Vajrayogini
shortDescription: Vajrayogini is a prominent female Buddhist tantric deity, considered a form of Shakti. She embodies the ultimate transformation of negative emotions into enlightened wisdom and compassion.
description: Vajrayogini is a central figure in Vajrayana Buddhism, particularly within the Chakrasamvara cycle of tantras. She is considered a form of Shakti, the dynamic feminine energy, and embodies the ultimate transformation of negative emotions, such as attachment and hatred, into enlightened wisdom and compassion. Her iconography is striking: she is typically depicted as a young, red-skinned woman, often naked or semi-naked, symbolizing her freedom from conventional limitations. She holds a curved knife (kartrika) in her right hand, representing the cutting away of ignorance, and a skull cup (kapala) filled with blood in her left, symbolizing the transmutation of negative energies into positive ones. A khatvanga (trident staff) rests on her shoulder, representing her consort and the union of wisdom and compassion. Vajrayogini is often depicted with a sow's head emerging from her ear, a symbol of her ability to transform even the most base instincts into enlightened awareness. Her practice is believed to lead to rapid spiritual progress and ultimate liberation. She is revered as a powerful protector and a source of profound wisdom and bliss, particularly for practitioners of tantra.

sources.title: Hidden Teachings of Tibet: An Explanation of the Terma Tradition of the Nyingma School of Buddhism
sources.author: Tulku Thondup Rinpoche
